en: PPE assessment must identify hazards and define required protective properties. PPE assessments must be repeated when assessment criteria change.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 70(5-6)\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 4(1)
PPE suitability must match workplace conditions and hazards.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 70(1)2-3\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 7(2)(3)
PPE provision must occur at employer expense when hazards cannot be otherwise controlled.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 69(2)\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 3(1)
PPE fit must be appropriate for the wearer, including necessary adjustments.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 70(1)5

PPE condition and hygiene must be maintained through cleaning, maintenance, repair, and replacement.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 17(1-2), 69(6)\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 3(5)
PPE information and instruction must occur before first PPE use and at least annually unless extended intervals are justified.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 7(1)
